title: Phase diagram of magnetic $S^3$ Skyrmions on three-dimensional lattices and the toroidal antiSkyrmion
authors: Niccolò Francini, Stefano Bolognesi, Sven Bjarke Gudnason, Roberto Menta
abstract: Magnetic Skyrmions are planar solitons stabilized by the Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction (DMI) and realized in chiral magnets. We study their natural three-dimensional generalization: a sigma model from $\mathbb{R}^3$ to $S^3$ with a four-component magnetization vector, stabilized by a one-derivative term which is a generalized DMI. We utilize two SO(3)-invariant generalized DMIs discovered recently: an "$α$-term" supporting a spherically symmetric hedgehog Skyrmion and a "$β$-term" supporting an axially symmetric Skyrmion that splits into two half-Skyrmions connected by a magnetic string of negative tension, a phenomenon we call "anti-confinement". We derive a cubic-lattice discretization that reproduces both continuum theories at long wavelengths and use Monte Carlo simulations to map the finite-temperature phase diagram. We identify spin-spiral, magnetic-string-lattice, Skyrmion-lattice, and antiSkyrmion-lattice phases, as well as a mixed-topology regime with fractional $S^3$ charges localized at string bends. We find, for the first time in the literature to the best of our knowledge, a toroidal (anti-)soliton of unit charge. Our results establish a theoretical and computational framework for three-dimensional topological magnetic textures in systems whose order-parameter manifold is $S^3$.
summary: This paper theoretically investigates magnetic Skyrmions in three dimensions using an S^3 order parameter model stabilized by generalized DMI interactions. By combining continuum theory with lattice simulations, the authors map a complex phase diagram revealing multiple ordered phases. Crucially, they establish the existence of stable toroidal anti-solitons and provide a comprehensive framework for 3D topological magnetic textures.
main_problem: The study investigates the natural three-dimensional generalization of magnetic Skyrmions, modeling them using a sigma model from R^3 to S^3.
main_result: The authors map out a complex phase diagram featuring spin-spiral, string lattice, and antiSkyrmion lattices, and find a stable toroidal anti-soliton with unit charge for the first time.
method: Methods include deriving continuum theories from generalized DMI terms, developing cubic-lattice discretizations, and employing Monte Carlo simulations to map finite-temperature phase diagrams.
model_or_system: The system is a magnetic material whose order parameter resides on the S^3 manifold. The energy functional includes Heisenberg exchange, Zeeman potential, and generalized Dzyaloshinskii–Moriya interactions ($\alpha$-term and $ eta$-term).
key_observables: Topological charges ($Q_{S^3}, Q_{S^2}$), phase boundaries (e.g., $B_{c1}, B_{c4}$), and the existence of toroidal anti-solitons.
important_parameters: DMI coupling constants ($\kappa$), external magnetic field ($B$), and temperature ($T$).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on deriving continuum limits from lattice discretizations and using approximations like spatial averaging for certain ground state calculations.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the dependence of topological charges ($Q_{S2}, Q_{S3}$) on external field $B$ and temperature, visualizing string lattices, Skyrmion lattices, and toroidal antiSkyrmions.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from defining the 3D sigma model and generalized DMI terms to deriving lattice discretizations. It then analyzes specific Ansätze (hedgehog/axially symmetric) to find soliton solutions, culminating in mapping out the full phase diagram via Monte Carlo simulations.
why_interesting: This work provides a rich theoretical framework for understanding topological magnetic textures beyond 2D systems, which is highly relevant for designing novel quantum materials and spintronic devices.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

Figure 3. Distribution of the 𝜋2(𝑆2) topological charge and 𝜋3(𝑆3) topological charge per site in a zero temperature configuration for 𝐿= 34 and 𝐵= 0.6. (a) The cut at 𝑧= 𝐿/2 exposes a topological charge per site 𝑞𝑆2 arranged in a triangular pattern, corresponding to the string lattice. (b) The stretch of the strings along the 𝑧-direction is suggested by the cut at 𝑦= 𝐿/2. In this case, 𝑞𝑆2 fills cylinders along the 𝑧-direction, corresponding to the strings. Each string has a concentration of 𝜋3(𝑆3) topological charge at its ends, representing the splitting of a Skyrmion into two halves and detached by the string in between them. The background spins not involved in the string lattice align...

Figure 4. Energy density comparison between a spin-spiral phase and a string lattice phase as a function of the Zeeman field 𝐵. The spin-spiral state is favored at low fields, up to 𝐵≈0.37, at which the string lattice becomes the lowest energy state. In this figure, the energy density of the string phase is estimated from a single string’s energy and size.

Figure 5. Finite-temperature phase diagram for the (a) 𝜋2(𝑆2) topo- logical charge and (b) 𝜋3(𝑆3) topological charge for the case with the 𝛼-term, together with zero-temperature iterative minimization re- sults for 𝐿= 16 and periodic boundary conditions. For large fields, 𝐵> 𝐵c4, the system is fully polarized at low temperatures. This phase is topologically trivial, with vanishing 𝜋2(𝑆2) and 𝜋3(𝑆3) topolog- ical charges. As the field decreases from large values, the system enters a low-temperature Skyrmion lattice phase, highlighted by the positive 𝜋3(𝑆3) topological charge, see red region in panel (b) for 𝐵c3 ≤𝐵≤𝐵c4.
